message: Your thought has been logged.

function.arguments:
{"thought": "The SWD debug scenario is running (vvp) and consuming 99.8% CPU. This means the swd_seq.vh is causing the simulation to run for a very long time. The issue is:

```verilog
    swclk = 1'b0;
    swdi  = 1'b1;
    repeat (60) begin
        @(posedge clk);
        swclk = ~swclk;
    end
```

This runs 60 clock cycles toggling swclk. That's fine. But then:

```verilog
    smoke_read32(32'h40000FFC, rd_data);  // GPIO0 ID via debug proxy
    smoke_read32(32'h40003FFC, rd_data);  // UART0 ID
    smoke_read32(32'h40015FFC, rd_data);  // CRC0 ID
    smoke_read32(32'h40016FFC, rd_data);  // AES0 ID
```

These reads might be timing out (4096 cycles each for APB access issues), consuming many cycles. 4 reads × 4096 = 16384 cycles, close to TB_MAX_CYCLES = 20000.

Then:
```verilog
    smoke_write32(32'h20001000, 32'hDECADE00); // write to SRAM
    smoke_read32(32'h20001000, rd_data);
```

SRAM operations that also timeout.

And the total cycle budget exceeds 20000, hitting the TB timeout message and $finish.

But actually, the vvp process is at 99.8% CPU which suggests it's actively computing, not stuck in a while loop. It might just be a long-running computation.

Let me just kill the process and fix the SWD seq to not do SRAM operations (since SRAM is hanging) and reduce the APB read count.
"}
function.name: think
execute_bash
execute_bash

message:
Running command: # Test SWD scenario quickly

# Test DEFAULT (systick/fw_boot scenarios)
iverilog -g2005-sv \
  -I.mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_socgen_tmp/tb \
  -fmspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/filelists/mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.f \
  -fmspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/tb/mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.f \
  -o /tmp/test_default.vvp 2>&1 | grep -c "error" | xargs -I{} echo "DEFAULT: compile errors={}"

# Test RAND_SYS  
iverilog -g2005-sv \
  -I.mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_socgen_tmp/tb \
  -DTEST_SEQ_RAND_SYS \
  -fmspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/filelists/mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.f \
  -fmspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/tb/mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.f \
  -o /tmp/test_rand_sys.vvp 2>&1 | grep -c "error" | xargs -I{} echo "RAND_SYS: compile errors={}"

# Test CONTENTION
iverilog -g2005-sv \
  -I.mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_socgen_tmp/tb \
  -DTEST_SEQ_CONTENTION \
  -fmspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/filelists/mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.f \
  -fmspm33c321a_derivative_mcu/tb/mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.f \
  -o /tmp/test_contention.vvp 2>&1 | grep -c "error" | xargs -I{} echo "CONTENTION: compile errors={}"

echo "all compiled"", "timeout": 120}

message:
Running command: # The runner isn't printing anything. Let me try debugging the runner directly
cd /workspace/nc-project-20260529-210526

python3 - << 'EOF'
import sys
sys.stdout.reconfigure(line_buffering=True)
sys.stderr.reconfigure(line_buffering=True)

import yaml
from pathlib import Path

design_root = Path('mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu')
manifest = yaml.safe_load((design_root / 'verification/manifest.yaml').read_text())

print("=== Header printed ===", flush=True)
print(f"scenarios: {len(manifest['scenarios'])}", flush=True)

# Quick test of the runner manually
import subprocess, time

build_dir = design_root / 'verification/sim_build'
socgen_tmp = design_root.parent / '.mspm33c321a_derivative_mcu_socgen_tmp'
rtl_f = design_root / 'filelists/mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.f'
tb_f = design_root / 'tb/mspm33c321a_derivative_compute_ss.f'

bin_path = build_dir / 'scenario_sys_reset_clock_TEST_SEQ_SYS_RESET.vvp'

if not bin_path.exists():
    print("Need to compile first...", flush=True)
    cmd = ['iverilog', '-g2005-sv', f'-I{socgen_tmp}/tb', '-DTEST_SEQ_SYS_RESET',
           f'-f{rtl_f}', f'-f{tb_f}', f'-o{bin_path}']
    t0 = time.time()
    r = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=180)
    print(f"Compile done in {time.time()-t0:.1f}s, rc={r.returncode}", flush=True)
else:
    print(f"Using cached: {bin_path}", flush=True)

print("Running simulation...", flush=True)
t0 = time.time()
r = subprocess.run([str(bin_path)], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=60,
                   cwd=str(design_root / 'fw/build'))
print(f"Sim done in {time.time()-t0:.1f}s, rc={r.returncode}", flush=True)
print("STDOUT:", r.stdout[-500:], flush=True)
EOF
